result.summary: sub_layer_0_02_context_agents contains 7 files implementing 2 main agents (Context Loading Agent and Layer 0 Orchestrator) using AALang/GAB patterns for context management and multi-agent orchestration

files.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
files.purpose: Design decisions and comparison with base AALang

files.name: context_loading_gab.jsonld
files.purpose: Context Loading Agent (4-mode-13-actor pattern) - loads and validates CLAUDE.md chain

files.name: layer_0_orchestrator.gab.jsonld
files.purpose: Layer 0 Universal Orchestrator (5-mode-15-actor pattern) - manages child agents across all layers

files.name: runtime/orchestrator_runtime.jsonld
files.purpose: Shared runtime behaviors for orchestrator agents

files.name: tests/integration_test.sh
files.purpose: Integration test script for context agents

agents.name: Context Loading Agent
agents.purpose: Load, validate, propagate, and deliver context for AI agents operating in the layer-stage system. Implements cross-session persistence and layer inheritance model.

agents.name: Layer 0 Orchestrator
agents.purpose: Universal orchestrator that receives tasks, delegates to child agents across layers, monitors progress, aggregates results, and reports to user/parent. Implements depth limits and circuit breakers for safety.

keyFeatures: AALang/GAB 4-mode and 5-mode patterns with Senior/Junior persona pairs
Layer inheritance model with override support and safety rule protection
Confidence-based mode transitions (thresholds: 0.6 for loading, 0.8 for work)
Multi-agent orchestration with depth limits (max 5) and concurrent limits (max 3)
Circuit breaker pattern for agent spawning
Hand-off documents protocol for inter-layer communication
content.purpose: These agents provide the foundational AI orchestration capabilities for the layer-stage system. The Context Loading Agent ensures AI agents understand their position and applicable rules before starting work. The Layer 0 Orchestrator enables hierarchical delegation and coordination of work across multiple AI agents.
